text: A symbiote. Alien. Alive. And desperate for a host. (page 3 panel 0)
Ben Grimm arrives at the Baxter Building searching for Reed — but finds something far more dangerous waiting beneath a sheet. (page 4 panel 2)
Reed explains the symbiote enhances its host's emotions, latching onto their conscience. Ben listens... too closely. (page 9 panel 0)
The symbiote speaks directly to Ben's deepest desire: to look human again. It offers him everything Reed cannot. (page 9 panel 1)
Ben's body begins to change. Black tendrils consume him. The Thing — becoming something else entirely. (page 14 panel 4)
Reed realizes the terrible truth: removing the symbiote means Ben loses his humanity forever. He calls it his responsibility to be a monster. (page 13 panel 3)
In the chaos, Venom — Ben's symbiote form — kills Reed Richards. Snaps his neck right in front of him. (page 28 panel 1)
Ben weeps over Reed's body, blaming himself. The symbiote whispers: there is no Fantastic Four. There is only us. (page 29 panel 2)
But Ben rejects the symbiote's words. With pure rage, he roars: It's just me. (page 29 panel 4)
We make monsters. We feed them our grief, our anger, our insecurity. And they hold up a mirror. (page 30 panel 3)
